text: The project attempts to instill independence and a spirit of entrepreneurship among students of Pondok Pesantren Nurul Amanah Bangkalan, Gresik through the Asset-Based Community Development (ABCD) model. For this research, participants were students of class 3 and 4, age ranging from 15 to 17 years old. Based on the field observation, they undergo training on skills that support business activity while they are practicing it by involving them in the business units that exist in their own madrasa such as Bakso Gibras, Nasi Ayam Chili Padi, water bottle, mushroom and catfish business. Theoretical knowledge of mu'amalah which is an integrated concept between Sharia economic education and entrepreneurship is taught and practiced by involving students to be involved directly in running the businesses. Results showed that students could participate and work actively in the business units; understand and master the ability to analyze how to deal with operational challenges and business opportunities. It can be concluded that by using an ABCD approach, students could acquire business skills and strengthen the social and religious values that underpin the existence of Pesantren, and that the student body could be empowered through participating in entrepreneurship activities while the school businesses were transformed into learning by doing experiences that can improve their economics.
